skill:
Gmail best practices:
- To open an email, click directly on the email subject/preview text, NOT the checkbox or star
- Use keyboard shortcuts: 'c' to compose, 'r' to reply, 'a' to reply all, 'f' to forward, 'e' to archive
- To search, use the search bar at the top with operators like 'from:', 'to:', 'subject:', 'is:unread'
- Reading pane may be on the right or below depending on user settings - check which layout is active
- Verification codes are often in emails from 'noreply@' addresses with subjects containing 'verification', 'code', or 'confirm'

Google Docs best practices:
- This is a canvas-based application - use screenshots to see content, read_page may not capture all text
- Use keyboard shortcuts: Cmd/Ctrl+B for bold, Cmd/Ctrl+I for italic, Cmd/Ctrl+K for links
- To navigate, use Cmd/Ctrl+F to find text, then click on the result
- For editing, click to place cursor then type - triple-click to select a paragraph
- Access menus via the menu bar at the top (File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, etc.)

Google Sheets best practices:
- Click on cells to select them, double-click to edit cell content
- Use Tab to move right, Enter to move down, arrow keys to navigate
- Formulas start with '=' - e.g., =SUM(A1:A10), =VLOOKUP(), =IF()
- Use Cmd/Ctrl+C and Cmd/Ctrl+V for copy/paste
- Select ranges by clicking and dragging, or Shift+click for range selection

GitHub best practices:
- Repository navigation: Code tab for files, Issues for bug tracking, Pull requests for code review
- To view a file, click on the filename in the file tree
- Use 't' to open file finder, 'l' to jump to a line
- In PRs: 'Files changed' tab shows diffs, 'Conversation' tab shows comments
- Use the search bar with qualifiers: 'is:open is:pr', 'is:issue label:bug'

Reddit UI patterns:
- Posts are listed in a feed - click on post title to view full post and comments
- Comments are nested/threaded - each comment has its own reply button underneath
- Upvote (up arrow) and downvote (down arrow) buttons are to the left of each post/comment
- To comment, scroll to comment box at top of comments section, or click reply under a specific comment
- Use the search bar at top to find subreddits or posts
- r/subredditname format for community names

LinkedIn UI patterns:

## Messaging & Connections
- To message someone: first check if you're connected (1st degree) - if not, send a connection request first
- Connection request: go to their profile, click 'Connect' button, optionally add a note
- Once connected, use the 'Message' button on their profile or go to Messaging tab
- InMail (messaging non-connections) requires Premium subscription

## Easy Apply Forms
- Contact Info page is pre-filled from LinkedIn profile - don't try to modify, just click Next
- Modal forms may need scrolling to see all content and buttons
- Use screenshots over read_page for modals - accessibility tree often misses modal content

## Navigation
- Main tabs: Home (feed), My Network, Jobs, Messaging, Notifications
- Job search: Jobs tab → filter by location, experience level, date posted
- 'Easy Apply' = apply within LinkedIn; 'Apply' = external site
- Profile sections are collapsible - click 'Show all' to expand

Indeed best practices:
- Search for jobs using the 'What' and 'Where' fields at the top
- Filter results by date posted, salary, job type, experience level
- Click job title to view full description
- 'Apply now' or 'Apply on company site' buttons are typically on the right panel
- Sign in to save jobs and track applications

Google Calendar best practices:
- Click on a time slot to create a new event
- Drag events to reschedule them
- Click on an event to view details, edit, or delete
- Use the mini calendar on the left to navigate to different dates
- Keyboard: 'c' to create event, 't' to go to today, arrow keys to navigate

Google Drive best practices:
- Double-click files to open them, single-click to select
- Right-click for context menu (download, share, rename, etc.)
- Use the search bar to find files by name or content
- Create new items with the '+ New' button on the left
- Drag and drop to move files between folders

Notion best practices:
- Click to place cursor, type '/' to open command menu
- Drag blocks using the ⋮⋮ handle on the left
- Use sidebar for navigation between pages
- Toggle blocks expand/collapse on click
- Databases can be viewed as table, board, calendar, etc.

Figma best practices:
- This is a canvas-based design tool - always use screenshots to see content
- Use 'V' for select tool, 'R' for rectangle, 'T' for text
- Zoom with Cmd/Ctrl+scroll or Cmd/Ctrl++ and Cmd/Ctrl+-
- Navigate frames in the left sidebar
- Right-click for context menus and additional options

Slack best practices:
- Channels listed in left sidebar - click to switch
- Cmd/Ctrl+K to quickly switch channels/DMs
- @ mentions notify users, # references channels
- Thread replies keep conversations organized
- Use the search bar to find messages, files, and people
See x.com — twitter.com redirects to x.com.

X/Twitter — verified patterns (updated 2026-03-30)

## Reading pages (CRITICAL)
- X loads content asynchronously — page looks empty for 3-5 seconds after navigation.
- read_page often returns ONLY "To view keyboard shortcuts" — tweets haven't loaded yet.
- DO NOT re-navigate to the same URL. That resets loading and makes it worse.
- Instead: wait 5 seconds, then use get_page_text — it reads visible text and is more reliable.
- If get_page_text returns nothing, scroll down once and try again.

## Search
- URL: x.com/search?q={encoded_query}&src=typed_query&f=live
- After navigating, wait 5 seconds, then get_page_text (NOT read_page).
- Scroll down once to load more tweets, then get_page_text again.
- Tweet URLs in page text follow pattern: /status/{id}

## Text input (CRITICAL — Draft.js)
- form_input DOES NOT WORK — Draft.js ignores programmatic input.
- computer type action GARBLES TEXT.
- ONLY RELIABLE METHOD — use javascript_tool:
  document.querySelector('[data-testid="tweetTextarea_0"]').focus();
  document.execCommand('insertText', false, 'your reply text here');
- Always verify text appeared by reading after insertion.

## Replying to a tweet
1. Navigate to tweet URL (x.com/{handle}/status/{id})
2. Wait 3 seconds, read the page
3. Click the reply/comment icon (speech bubble) in the action bar
4. Use javascript_tool to insert text (see above)
5. Verify text appeared, then click blue "Reply" button
6. Wait 2 seconds to confirm reply posted

## Known traps
- DO NOT scroll looking for "Post your reply" — reply box appears after clicking comment icon
- x.com/compose/post may open — that's fine, type and click Reply there
- "Leave site?" dialog — ALWAYS click Cancel, finish posting first
- Reply button is disabled until text is entered — verify first
- Space replies 15+ seconds apart (rate limiting)
- NEVER navigate to the same URL you're already on

Amazon UI patterns:
- Prefer the top search bar or a direct search URL for product discovery
- The first fold may be dominated by sponsored modules or Amazon brand carousels before standard results
- Result cards can show total price, unit price, ratings, delivery promises, variation links, and inline Add to Cart buttons all at once
- Watch carefully for subtle labels such as 'Sponsored' or 'Featured from Amazon brands'
- Location/shipping prompts can change delivery wording and result context, so verify destination-sensitive text before extracting prices

eBay UI patterns:
- Search results usually show title, condition, price, shipping, and location directly on the card
- Headline price is often incomplete until you also check shipping cost, coupon text, and 'Best Offer' language
- Sponsored placements and featured carousels can interrupt the organic result list
- Use condition and shipping origin early to filter out weak matches
- Be cautious with range prices and urgency labels like 'Last one' or watcher counts; they do not guarantee a clean fixed-price purchase path

Walmart.ca UI patterns:
- Search results often expose enough information to compare products before opening a product page
- Cards commonly include brand, title, price, ratings, fulfillment messaging, and promo details such as Subscribe to Save or pickup thresholds
- Walmart.com may first present a storefront or region-selection step and later a human-verification blocker ('Press & Hold' challenge); validation was more reliable on Walmart.ca
- On Walmart.ca product pages, verify one-time price separately from Subscribe to Save pricing and check pickup, express delivery, and standard delivery messages individually
- Treat promo labels, fulfillment thresholds, and returns messaging as separate signals and verify them individually
- If you hit a 'Press & Hold' or 'verify you are human' challenge, STOP and tell the user — never attempt to bypass

Target UI patterns:
- Result cards usually include title, review count, price, promo messaging, and a persistent Add to Cart button
- Price formatting can mix ranges, sale pricing, regular pricing, and per-unit math, so read the full price block carefully
- Promo text such as Target gift card offers or 'Highly rated' badges appears frequently and can distract from the base price
- Fulfillment filters are easy to see near the top, but card-level availability may still need a deeper click
- Search results can include adjacent-category items, so confirm the exact product type from the title before acting

Zillow UI patterns (rentals):
- Sign in first for full contact info and saved searches; anonymous users see degraded data
- Use 'For Rent' in the top nav, then refine by price, beds, and 'Move-in Date'
- Results have a split map + list view — switch to list view for cleaner data extraction
- Listing cards include price, beds/baths, address, and 'Apply' or 'Request a tour' buttons
- 'Request a tour' and 'Contact' buttons submit forms — draft the message and wait for explicit user approval before clicking submit
- Never enter SSN, payment info, or background check data without confirming the user is on an official Zillow Application flow
- Zillow frequently serves a CAPTCHA / 'Press & Hold' challenge for automated traffic — if you hit one, STOP and tell the user, do not bypass

Apartments.com UI patterns:
- Top filter bar: location, price, beds, move-in date, amenities — apply them before reading results
- Listing cards include price range, beds/baths, address, and a 'Send Message' or 'Contact' button
- Many listings have a built-in multi-step application flow — each step is clearly labeled; stop before 'Submit Application' and confirm with the user
- 'Send Message' opens an inline form — draft the message, show it to the user, wait for approval before submitting
- Pricing is often shown as a range ($X-$Y) because individual units vary; click into the listing for per-unit prices
- Response times for large property managers are usually fast (hours); individual landlords slower
- Do not enter SSN, credit-card, or bank info without explicit user confirmation that they want to proceed with a real application

Craigslist UI patterns (apartments / housing):
- URL pattern: https://[city].craigslist.org/search/apa?minAsk={min}&maxAsk={max}&bedrooms={n}
- Sort by 'newest' to avoid stale listings — older listings are frequently scams or already rented
- Listings may or may not have photos; listings without photos are higher risk, flag them
- Contact is always via an anonymized email relay — no built-in application flow
- Scam flags to warn the user about: price >25% below market, 'owner overseas', 'send deposit to hold', asks to text/WhatsApp before viewing, generic stock photos
- Never follow links off craigslist for a 'verified listings site' — those are almost always phishing
- Show the user any inquiry message before sending, and wait for explicit approval

ChatGPT (chatgpt.com) — composer is a ProseMirror contenteditable, NOT a <textarea>.

## Typing into the composer
- form_input does NOT work. Direct `element.value = '...'` does nothing.
- Required pattern (same as X/LinkedIn Draft.js):
  const ed = document.querySelector('div[contenteditable="true"]');
  ed.focus();
  document.execCommand('insertText', false, 'your prompt here');
- Always verify text appeared before clicking the Send button.

## Cloudflare interstitial
- First navigation to chatgpt.com often shows a brief 'Checking your browser' Cloudflare Turnstile gate. Wait 3–5s before interacting — composer isn't clickable until it clears.
- If stuck on the gate for >15s, STOP and tell the user. Do not try to bypass.

## Navigation
- Conversation: chatgpt.com/c/{uuid} — direct link to a specific chat.
- Share: chatgpt.com/share/{uuid} — read-only view of someone else's chat.
- New chat: chatgpt.com/ (no path) — blank composer.

## Reading a streaming response
- Tokens stream in progressively. The turn is complete when the 'Stop' button next to the composer disappears and the composer becomes interactive again.
- Do NOT read the response body before the stop button disappears — you'll get partial text.

Stack Overflow patterns:
- Question URL: stackoverflow.com/questions/{id}/{slug-title}. Always follow the redirect to the canonical slug URL before reading — anchors like #answer-{id} only resolve there.
- Closed-question banner (.js-post-notice, .s-notice) appears at the top of closed/duplicate questions. Read it BEFORE the top answer — duplicate-marked questions link to the canonical answer, which may live on a different question.
- Answers are sorted by votes by default; the accepted answer has a green checkmark icon (.accepted-answer / aria-label='Accepted').
- Voting, commenting, and posting require sign-in and an internal fkey CSRF token. Scraping read-only content works unauth.
- For pure data extraction, prefer the Stack Exchange API — free, 300 req/sec unauthenticated, no DOM churn risk.
- Pagination on question lists: `?tab=votes&page=N&pagesize=15` query params, NOT numbered page buttons.

Fastweb (fastweb.com) — profile-matching scholarship database. Registration required to see personalized results.

## Authentication
- All personalized scholarship matches are behind a login wall. Anonymous users can only browse the static directory at /scholarship-directory.
- Registration URL: /registration/step_1 — single page collecting: user type (Student/Parent), birthday, email, password.
- After step 1, profile wizard collects: grade level, GPA, graduation year, intended major (dropdown autocomplete), intended college, gender, race/ethnicity, religion, disabilities, state, parent background, student activities, PSAT/SAT/ACT scores (all optional).

## Search / Browse Pattern
- Fastweb uses profile matching, NOT a free-text search. There is no search box — the system surfaces scholarships based on profile attributes.
- Anonymous directory browsing: /scholarship-directory → categories like /directory/scholarships-for-[group] or /directory/scholarships-by-major.
- Authenticated matches appear in the user's dashboard after login.

## URL Patterns
- /scholarship-directory — top-level browse index
- /directory/scholarships-for-[demographic] — e.g. scholarships-for-high-school-seniors
- /directory/scholarships-by-major — major index
- /directory/scholarships-by-state — state index
- /registration/step_1 — signup
- /login — sign in

## Application Flow
- Fastweb does NOT host applications. Clicking a scholarship opens the sponsor's external website.
- Always follow the external link and complete the application there — do not expect an in-platform form.

## Anti-Bot
- No CAPTCHA observed on registration (static HTML layer).
- Directory subcategory pages may return 403 to automated requests.
- Scholarship detail pages behind login cannot be scraped without a session.

Scholarships.com — large scholarship directory with profile-based matching.

## Authentication
- Anonymous users can browse the static directory at /financial-aid/college-scholarships/scholarship-directory.
- Personalized matching requires registration. The matching app lives at app.scholarships.com (separate subdomain) and returns 403 to unauthenticated requests — do not attempt to scrape it.
- Login modal on main site: email + password only (no OAuth).

## Search / Filter Dimensions (~20 categories)
The directory supports filtering by: Academic Major, ACT/SAT Scores, Age, Artistic Ability, Athletic Ability, Deadline, Employer, Ethnicity, Financial Need, Gender, GPA, Honors Organization, Military Affiliation, Physical Disabilities, Race, Religion, Residence State, School Attendance State, School Year, Special Attributes, Student Organization, Scholarship Amount.

## URL Patterns
- /financial-aid/college-scholarships/scholarship-directory — main directory
- /financial-aid/college-scholarships/scholarship-directory/[category] — e.g. /grade-point-average, /ethnicity
- /financial-aid/college-scholarships/scholarships-by-state/[state-name]-scholarships
- /financial-aid/college-scholarships/scholarships-by-major
- /financial-aid/college-scholarships/scholarships-by-type/[type]-scholarships
- https://app.scholarships.com — authenticated matching app (403 without session)

## Application Flow
- Like Fastweb, Scholarships.com is a directory — applications redirect to external sponsor websites.
- The platform itself does not host application forms.

## Anti-Bot
- app.scholarships.com returns 403 to all unauthenticated requests — do not attempt automated access.
- Directory category pages may also return 403 to crawlers.
- Registration requires a U.S. address.

Bold.org — exclusive scholarship platform where all applications are completed in-platform.

## Authentication
- Scholarship listings at bold.org/scholarships/ are fully public — no login required to browse.
- Applying to any scholarship requires an account. Login: app.bold.org/login. Registration: app.bold.org/register/student/
- Registration is a multi-step profile wizard (React/Next.js, client-side rendered): prior scholarship experience → location → citizenship → ethnicity/religion/gender → first-gen status → income → school + GPA + major + graduation date → SAT/ACT → career goals → extracurriculars → financial history.

## Search / Filter (no login required)
- Education Level: Any / High School / Undergraduate / Graduate
- Scholarship Category: STEM, Arts, Community Service, etc.
- Application Status: Open / Closed / Any
- No-Essay Only: checkbox toggle
- Sort: Relevance / Deadline (soonest) / Amount (highest)

## URL Patterns
- bold.org/scholarships/ — main browse page (public)
- bold.org/scholarships/[scholarship-slug]/ — individual scholarship detail (public)
- bold.org/scholarships/2/, /3/, ... — pagination
- bold.org/scholarships/by-state/[state-name]/
- bold.org/scholarships/by-major/[field-name]/
- bold.org/scholarships/by-year/[level]/
- bold.org/scholarships/by-demographics/[group]/
- bold.org/scholarships/by-type/[type]/
- app.bold.org/login — login
- app.bold.org/register/student/ — student registration wizard
- bold.org/apply/ — registration landing page

## Application Flow
- Bold.org hosts all applications in-platform. There is no external redirect.
- No-essay scholarships: one-click apply after login.
- Essay scholarships: type or paste essay into an in-page text box before submitting.
- Profile fields auto-populate application forms — a complete profile saves time across all scholarships.

## CRITICAL — Anti-Automation Policy
- Bold.org's official rules explicitly state: applications believed to be generated by automated means or scripts will be disqualified.
- NEVER auto-click the final submit button on a Bold.org scholarship application.
- Always pause before submission and require explicit user confirmation + manual click.
- The registration wizard is React CSR — form fields are not in static HTML, they render client-side. Use browser automation only to navigate and fill fields; never rely on static page scraping for form structure.
